description:
## Background

Unit tests can prove the Markdown and Diagram component contracts while mocking Mermaid and pan/zoom. They cannot prove that the built Tauri desktop webview dynamically loads Mermaid, mounts generated SVG, applies the Beadsmith Theme, and handles real user-authored Issue content. This slice closes that integration gap.

## What to build

Extend the real desktop Issue Detail acceptance path with representative Issue description and comment Mermaid fixtures. Prove valid Diagram rendering, pan/zoom/reset interaction, Source access, and malformed-source recovery through the compiled Beadsmith binary.

## Technical Approach

- Reuse the documented disposable Beadwork Workspace and embedded WebDriver architecture in `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md`; do not create a renderer-only browser test.
- Add a representative valid Mermaid fence to both an Issue description and a comment, plus a malformed Mermaid fixture that preserves its raw source.
- Drive the normal Issue selection path in the real built app. Assert the rendered SVG, tab semantics, pan/zoom controls, reset behavior, Source contents, and complete error banner/fallback.
- Prefer semantic roles, accessible names, and stable fixture text. Do not add brittle pixel snapshots.
- Add or document a focused command alongside the existing WebDriver suite.

## Considerations

- Keep fixtures disposable and created through Beadwork, never by editing persisted Issue/store files.
- The current suite targets Issue List behavior; reuse its helpers where clear, but keep Mermaid-specific scenario ownership and failure output focused.
- A missing SVG, broken dynamic chunk, or Tauri-webview incompatibility is a real blocker: do not replace this proof with a jsdom-only assertion.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The real built Tauri app renders a valid Mermaid Diagram from an Issue description and from a comment.
- [ ] The scenario proves pan, Ctrl/Command-wheel zoom or its deterministic command equivalent, reset/fit, and Source access.
- [ ] A malformed fixture exposes the complete error banner and unchanged Source in the real app.
- [ ] The test uses disposable Beadwork fixtures and the normal Issue Detail path.
- [ ] A focused documented command runs the scenario, and existing desktop Issue Explorer coverage remains green.

## Test Plan

- Build with the repository's established desktop E2E command.
- Run the focused Mermaid scenario repeatedly against a fresh fixture.
- Run existing Issue Explorer desktop scenarios plus frontend type/lint checks for harness changes.
title: Prove Mermaid Diagrams in the real desktop Issue Detail
